name: Maine-et-Loire (France)
description: Per pale: at the hoist a blue square bearing two yellow fleurs-de-lis within a red border (arms of Anjou); at the fly a blue field charged with a red Cross of Lorraine (double cross) fimbriated yellow.
history: The flag combines the Angevin arms (Anjou) with the Cross of Lorraine, emblems associated with the historic province of Anjou.
